plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
plan.goal: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Capture migration invariants and baseline the current failure mode
node.summary: Measure the current co-change growth path, define the authoritative-versus-derived boundaries, and lock in the invariants the rewrite must preserve before implementation work branches.

acceptance.label: Documents the current pathological path from refresh to co-change persistence to daemon health degradation
Defines the target separation between authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence
Captures baseline measurements for cache growth, startup load, refresh latency, and daemon RSS

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
plan.goal: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Add immediate guardrails against pathological co-change batches
node.summary: Introduce short-term containment so large change sets stop producing quadratic co-change writes while the deeper architecture migration is in progress.

acceptance.label: Large or bulk refreshes no longer emit pathological co-change delta volumes on the hot path
Containment behavior is explicit and observable in logs or diagnostics
The daemon remains responsive while the broader migration is still incomplete

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
plan.goal: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Remove cold co-change history from hot runtime hydration
node.summary: Refactor startup and refresh hydration so the live daemon loads only authoritative runtime state plus bounded serving projections, never the full historical co-change corpus.

acceptance.label: Daemon startup no longer hydrates unbounded history co-change rows into in-memory runtime state
Hot runtime memory is bounded by authoritative state plus serving projections rather than cold analytical history
Startup and restart latency improve measurably on an existing large cache DB

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
plan.goal: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Introduce a cold evidence model and bounded projection materialization pipeline
node.summary: Replace unbounded persisted pairwise co-change history with a colder evidence representation and a separate path that derives only the bounded serving projection needed by MCP queries.

acceptance.label: Authoritative state and analytical co-change evidence are persisted in separate layers with clear ownership
Serving-side co-change projection remains bounded and can be refreshed without full historical hydration
The new model preserves enough evidence to derive co-change behavior without quadratic hot-path writes

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
plan.goal: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Migrate and compact legacy cache state safely
node.summary: Provide a migration path that retires or compacts legacy history_co_change data, reclaims dead SQLite pages, and leaves old workspaces on a healthy state shape after upgrade.

acceptance.label: Existing oversized cache DBs can migrate to the new shape without losing authoritative runtime correctness
Legacy co-change data is compacted, retired, or rebuilt according to the new architecture
Post-migration database size and freelist growth are materially reduced

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Harden refresh, locking, and freshness behavior around long-running persistence work
node.summary: Ensure the background runtime and request paths degrade gracefully when projection or migration work is in flight instead of surfacing unhealthy daemon states from lock contention.

acceptance.label: Background refresh no longer repeatedly fails with database-locked errors during expected long-running maintenance or projection work
Daemon health and freshness reporting distinguish bounded lag from hard failure
Request paths remain orientation-giving even while derived state is catching up

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.

node.title: Validate the migration against daemon health, performance, and correctness
node.summary: Exercise startup, restart, refresh, query, and migration flows on realistic cache states and confirm the new architecture fixes health degradation without regressing correctness.

acceptance.label: Representative tests cover startup, restart, refresh, and migration behavior under large historical state
Measured daemon RSS, cache DB growth, and refresh latency improve against the recorded baseline
No correctness regressions appear in lineage, projection, or query behavior after migration

node.title: Capture migration invariants and baseline the current failure mode
node.summary: Baseline and migration invariants are captured in docs/CO_CHANGE_RUNTIME_REWRITE.md, including current cache and daemon metrics, the pathological 3.49M co-change delta refresh, and the required boundary split between authoritative state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence.

acceptance.label: Documents the current pathological path from refresh to co-change persistence to daemon health degradation
Defines the target separation between authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence
Captures baseline measurements for cache growth, startup load, refresh latency, and daemon RSS

node.title: Add immediate guardrails against pathological co-change batches
node.summary: The co-change rewrite removed the hot-path source of pathological pairwise writes, so large refreshes no longer persist unbounded symbol-pair history and daemon responsiveness no longer depends on short-term guardrail skips.

acceptance.label: Large or bulk refreshes no longer emit pathological co-change delta volumes on the hot path
Containment behavior is explicit and observable in logs or diagnostics
The daemon remains responsive while the broader migration is still incomplete

node.title: Remove cold co-change history from hot runtime hydration
node.summary: Startup hydration now loads authoritative history and bounded serving projections only; legacy unbounded co-change history is no longer hydrated into the live daemon, which materially reduced restart memory and startup latency on the previously oversized cache DB.

acceptance.label: Daemon startup no longer hydrates unbounded history co-change rows into in-memory runtime state
Hot runtime memory is bounded by authoritative state plus serving projections rather than cold analytical history
Startup and restart latency improve measurably on an existing large cache DB

node.title: Introduce a cold evidence model and bounded projection materialization pipeline
node.summary: Unbounded persisted pairwise co-change history was removed from authoritative state; the store now keeps plain lineage history and derives bounded serving co-change from colder evidence instead of hydrating or persisting an unbounded historical pair table.

acceptance.label: Authoritative state and analytical co-change evidence are persisted in separate layers with clear ownership
Serving-side co-change projection remains bounded and can be refreshed without full historical hydration
The new model preserves enough evidence to derive co-change behavior without quadratic hot-path writes

node.title: Migrate and compact legacy cache state safely
node.summary: Store open now retires legacy history_co_change state, records the migration, and vacuums when reclaim is large enough; on the live cache this shrank the DB from roughly 2.25 GB to roughly 278 MB while preserving startup correctness.

acceptance.label: Existing oversized cache DBs can migrate to the new shape without losing authoritative runtime correctness
Legacy co-change data is compacted, retired, or rebuilt according to the new architecture
Post-migration database size and freelist growth are materially reduced

node.title: Harden refresh, locking, and freshness behavior around long-running persistence work
node.summary: Runtime refresh and freshness now treat bounded lag as a deferred state instead of a daemon failure: workspace-runtime snapshot loaders use pure-read session helpers, transient SQLite lock contention in the background refresh loop is downgraded to deferred refresh bookkeeping, and runtimeStatus reports health ok while freshness surfaces deferred lag explicitly.

acceptance.label: Background refresh no longer repeatedly fails with database-locked errors during expected long-running maintenance or projection work
Daemon health and freshness reporting distinguish bounded lag from hard failure
Request paths remain orientation-giving even while derived state is catching up

node.title: Validate the migration against daemon health, performance, and correctness
node.summary: The migration has now been validated end to end: required prism-js and prism-mcp checks passed on the rewritten co-change architecture, the release prism-cli/prism-mcp build succeeded, the restarted daemon is healthy, and native plan completion bookkeeping now accepts task-correlated validation outcomes for native node ids without requiring anchors or a backing coordination task.

acceptance.label: Representative tests cover startup, restart, refresh, and migration behavior under large historical state
Measured daemon RSS, cache DB growth, and refresh latency improve against the recorded baseline
No correctness regressions appear in lineage, projection, or query behavior after migration

plan.title: Rewrite PRISM co-change persistence and refresh materialization boundaries so authoritative runtime state, bounded serving projections, and cold analytical evidence are separated; eliminate pathological co-change growth from the daemon hot path; and migrate existing cache state without regressing correctness, startup time, or daemon health.
